name: Monte Igeldo
description: This difficult 32.1-mile touring cycling route from Zarauz offers challenging climbs, stunning coastal views, and historic landmarks.
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Zarautz traverse a diverse landscape, from the extensive coastline along the Bay of Biscay to verdant hills and vineyards. The region features the long Zarautz Beach and inland areas with natural parks such as Pagoeta. Cyclists encounter varied terrain, including coastal promenades and challenging ascents with significant elevation gains. This area provides a mix of paved roads and some unpaved segments, offering routes for different fitness levels. Best touring cycling routes around Zarautz The most popular touring cycling route is Monte Igeldo, a 32.1 miles (51.7 km) trail that takes 3 hours 34 minutes to complete. This route features significant climbs and coastal views. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is View of the Bay of Biscay – View of Zarautz Bay loop from San Pelaio, a difficult 28.2 miles (45.4 km) path. This route offers panoramic views of the coastline and Zarautz Bay. Local touring cyclists also love the Guetaria - Getaria – Ratón de Getaria loop from Zarautz, a 9.3 miles (14.9 km) trail leading through coastal scenery towards Getaria, often completed in about 1 hour. Touring cycling around Zarautz is defined by coastal paths, green hills, and vineyards, offering routes that range from leisurely rides to challenging mountain ascents. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy family-friendly paths to difficult climbs. The routes in Zarautz are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 150 reviews. More than 2,000 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Zarautz's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Zarautz?
A: Zarautz offers a wide selection of touring cycling routes, with over 45 different options to explore. These routes cater to various preferences, from coastal paths to challenging mountain ascents.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Zarautz?
A: The terrain around Zarautz is incredibly diverse, ranging from extensive coastlines along the Bay of Biscay to verdant hills and vineyards. You'll find a mix of paved roads and some unpaved segments, with routes featuring everything from leisurely coastal promenades to challenging ascents with significant elevation gains, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1079163488/monte-igeldo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monte Igeldo&lt;/a&gt; loop.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Zarautz?
A: June and August are particularly popular months for cycling in Zarautz, offering favorable weather conditions to enjoy the diverse landscapes. However, the region's mild climate generally allows for touring cycling throughout much of the year.

Q: Are there options for less experienced touring cyclists or families in Zarautz?
A: Yes, Zarautz provides routes suitable for all skill levels. While some routes demand very good fitness, many feature mostly paved surfaces and manageable climbs. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31480383" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guetaria - Getaria – Ratón de Getaria loop from Zarautz&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 9.3-mile path that offers pleasant coastal scenery.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while cycling around Zarautz?
A: While cycling, you can encounter several notable natural features and landmarks. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/852732" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Algorri Flysch&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1101604" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Zumaia Flysch Cliffs&lt;/a&gt; are geological wonders. You might also pass by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4644715" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Telmo Hermitage&lt;/a&gt; or the iconic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5095732" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ratón de Getaria&lt;/a&gt;. The protected biotope of Iñurritza, with its estuary and sand dunes, is another unique area to explore.

Q: Are there specific viewpoints offering great panoramas along the routes?
A: Absolutely. Many routes offer breathtaking panoramas. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20524452" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Bay of Biscay – View of Zarautz Bay loop from San Pelaio&lt;/a&gt; is specifically designed for stunning coastal and bay views. Additionally, the Santa Bárbara Viewpoint and Mount Talaimendi provide spellbinding panoramic views of the entire bay, the town, and the rugged Basque coastline.

Q: Are there any challenging touring cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Yes, Zarautz is well-suited for experienced cyclists seeking a challenge. Routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1079163488/monte-igeldo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monte Igeldo&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38296541" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Luzarbe Recreation Area – View of the Bay of Biscay loop from San Pelaio&lt;/a&gt; feature significant climbs and elevation gains, offering a demanding yet rewarding experience with spectacular views.

Q: Can I find routes that combine coastal views with vineyards?
A: Yes, the region around Zarautz is known for its unique landscape where vineyards meet the sea. You can find trails that wind through these areas, offering a distinct sensory experience that combines the beauty of the coastline with the charm of local viticulture.

Q: What do other cyclists say about touring cycling in Zarautz?
A: The touring cycling routes around Zarautz are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 150 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse terrain, the stunning coastal and mountain views, and the variety of routes available for different fitness levels.

Q: Are there circular touring cycling routes available in Zarautz?
A: Yes, many of the touring cycling routes in Zarautz are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20524452" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Bay of Biscay – View of Zarautz Bay loop from San Pelaio&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31480383" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guetaria - Getaria – Ratón de Getaria loop from Zarautz&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: How accessible are the touring cycling routes in Zarautz by public transport?
A: Zarautz is a well-connected town in the Basque Country. While specific public transport options for each route vary, the town itself is accessible by train and bus, making it a convenient starting point for many cycling adventures. It's advisable to check local transport schedules for routes that might start further afield.

Q: Where can I park my car when starting a touring cycling route in Zarautz?
A: Zarautz offers various parking options within the town, especially near the beach and main areas. For routes starting slightly outside the town center, such as those from San Pelaio, there are often designated parking spots available. It's recommended to check local parking regulations and availability, especially during peak season.

From this point of the path you will be able to observe incredible views towards the outcrops of the Cantabrian coast. You can also take advantage of the afternoon to contemplate dreamy sunsets, especially in autumn.

The climb to Igedo is not easy, but it does have its rewards. These fabulous views to the west of Donostia-San Sebastián, the church of the Diocesan Seminary and, in the distance, the Basque hills will leave you speechless.

faqs.question: How difficult is the Monte Igeldo circular cycling route?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as difficult for touring cyclists, requiring very good fitness. It features significant elevation changes with approximately 890 meters of ascent and descent over 51.7 kilometers. Some segments may be steep enough to require pushing your bike.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route primarily features mostly paved surfaces that are generally in very good condition. While the main roads are well-maintained, some challenging sections in the region might include unpaved parts. Traffic tends to be light for much of the year.

faqs.question: What are the main sights and viewpoints along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter numerous stunning viewpoints. Highlights include the View of Zarautz Bay, Igeldo, and the Viewpoint Over Getaria and San Sebastián. The route also offers impressive views of La Concha Bay and the wider Bay of Biscay, with panoramic vistas from Monte Igeldo itself.

faqs.question: When is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The trail offers beautiful scenery throughout the year, but for the most comfortable cycling experience, consider visiting during spring or autumn when temperatures are milder and the weather is generally more stable. Clear days offer the best chance to see as far as France from Monte Igeldo.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start this cycling route?
faqs.answer: While specific parking details for Zarauz are not provided, Zarauz is a well-known coastal town. You should be able to find public parking options within the town to begin your circular route. It's advisable to check local parking regulations upon arrival.

faqs.question: Can I reach the starting point by public transport?
faqs.answer: Zarauz is accessible by public transport, including train and bus services, making it possible to reach the starting point without a car. Check local transport schedules for the most up-to-date information on routes and timings to Zarauz.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this route?
faqs.answer: No specific permits or entrance fees are required to cycle the Monte Igeldo circular route itself. However, if you plan to visit the historic amusement park at the summit of Monte Igeldo, there will be an entrance fee for the park and its attractions.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the route is primarily on paved roads, it's generally not recommended for dogs due to the challenging nature of the ride and potential traffic. If you do bring a dog, ensure it is well-trained, on a leash, and capable of handling the distance and elevation changes, or consider alternative, less strenuous routes.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, portions of this route intersect with the historic Camino de Santiago, specifically the Northern Route (Basque Country). It also crosses sections of the Bidegorri and the Donostia - Mutriku trails.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or drink along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, you'll find opportunities for refreshments. Zarauz itself offers various cafes and pubs. Additionally, the summit of Monte Igeldo, with its amusement park, provides facilities where you can find food and drinks.

faqs.question: What is a unique highlight or insider tip for this route?
faqs.answer: Beyond the stunning views, a unique highlight is the historic amusement park at the summit of Monte Igeldo. Opened in 1912, it features a vintage funicular and attractions like the 'Swiss Mountain,' one of the oldest operating steel roller coasters globally. It's a charming step back in time and a great reward after the climb.
